steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Transitions question. The blank must connect two sentences that disagree: sentence 1 says humans arrived ~13,000 years ago; sentence 2 presents new evidence suggesting 30,000+ years ago — “much earlier than previously thought.” The correct transition must signal contrast or contradiction.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘As a result,’: signals that what follows is a consequence of what came before. The new finding is not a result of the old belief — it contradicts it. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Similarly,’: signals that what follows parallels or resembles what came before. The two theories diverge sharply (13,000 vs. 30,000 years), so similarity is wrong. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘However,’: signals contrast or contradiction. The new radiocarbon-based research directly contradicts the long-held 13,000-year view, making this the right signal. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘In conclusion,’: signals a summary or final statement. The second sentence introduces a new finding, not a conclusion drawn from the first. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
